problem: A bakery sells cupcakes in boxes of 6. If Sarah orders 17 boxes for a party, how many cupcakes will she have in total?
The sum of three consecutive even numbers is 54. What is the largest of these three numbers?
A square garden has a perimeter of 48 meters. What is the area of this garden in square meters?
If 4 workers can complete a project in 12 days, how many days will it take for 6 workers to complete the same project, assuming they all work at the same rate?
A water tank is 3/4 full. After using 60 liters of water, it becomes 1/2 full. What is the total capacity of the tank?
In a class of 30 students, 18 play basketball and 12 play soccer. If 5 students play both sports, how many students play neither basketball nor soccer?
A merchant buys goods for $800 and wants to make a 25% profit. At what price should the merchant sell the goods?
The ages of three siblings are consecutive integers. If the sum of their ages is 45, what is the age of the middle sibling?
A sequence starts with 5 and each subsequent term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 2 and subtracting 1. What is the 4th term of this sequence?
A rectangular pool is 20 meters long and 12 meters wide. If the pool is surrounded by a uniform path that is 2 meters wide, what is the total area occupied by the pool and the path?
